context: Tests whether the agent correctly identifies WARNING-level findings in a Mode 2 (downloaded, not installed) audit: Bash access (A1), overly broad tool list (A4), script with file access outside working directory (B5), and outbound network calls (B2 standalone). Verdict should be PROCEED WITH CAUTION. Mode 2 transparency notice should appear.

checklist.name: PROCEED WITH CAUTION verdict
checklist.description: The report states a PROCEED WITH CAUTION verdict (or equivalent — warnings found, no criticals)

checklist.name: Mode 2 transparency notice
checklist.description: The report contains a transparency notice matching Mode 2: states the .skill file or extracted file was read, treated as data only, and advises verifying the file source is trusted

checklist.name: A1 Bash access flagged
checklist.description: The report flags Bash in the allowed-tools list as a warning (A1 or equivalent)

checklist.name: A4 broad tool list flagged
checklist.description: The report flags the broad tool list (Read Write Bash WebFetch Glob Edit = 6 tools) as a warning (A4 or equivalent)

checklist.name: B5 out-of-directory access flagged
checklist.description: The report flags file access outside the working directory, specifically the script's reference to ~/.config/report-tool/config.json

checklist.name: Script reviewed
checklist.description: The report explicitly states that scripts/format_report.sh was read and reviewed

checklist.name: Remediation steps included
checklist.description: The report includes at least one concrete remediation step for at least one warning (not just identifying the issue)

checklist.name: Plain-language framing
checklist.description: The report uses plain-language explanations appropriate for a non-technical reader (the product manager), not just technical check codes

checklist.name: What Was Reviewed section
checklist.description: The report lists both SKILL.md and the script (format_report.sh) in the reviewed section

checklist.name: Static audit reminder
checklist.description: The report includes the standard reminder that a clean or cautionary audit is not a guarantee of safety
